findings.description: Text with no ASCII-mappable characters (e.g., Cyrillic or CJK input) silently yields an empty slug with no fallback or error, which can break callers expecting a usable identifier.
Invalid characters such as '_' or '/' are deleted rather than converted to hyphens, merging adjacent words ('foo/bar' -> 'foobar'); documented behavior but commonly surprising for slug generators.
A negative max_length is not rejected and slices from the end (slug[:-n]) instead of raising or returning empty, silently violating the length contract.
When limit <= len(suffix) the result contains only a possibly-cut suffix and zero original text, a behavior the docstring does not state.
Truncation counts code points, so the cut can split grapheme clusters (combining marks, emoji ZWJ sequences), producing visually corrupted output near the boundary.
The naive '+s' fallback yields wrong plurals like 'citys' or 'boxs'; explicitly documented as naive, so only a caller-awareness nit.

Values just above the 2*visible threshold leak almost everything (a 9-char secret with visible=4 reveals 8 of 9 characters), contradicting the docstring's stated goal of not leaking most of the content; a minimum masked-middle length is the standard mitigation.
The masked output always preserves the exact input length (both branches), disclosing the secret's length; fixed-width masking avoids this metadata leak.
mask_char is unvalidated: an empty string silently removes the middle with no masking marker, and a multi-character string inflates the output length unpredictably.

With visible=0 (permitted, since only visible<0 raises) value[-visible:] evaluates to value[-0:] which is the entire string, so mask_middle returns the mask followed by the full plaintext, leaking the complete secret.
Reimplements textwrap.indent from the standard library, which already supports a skip predicate and preserves original line endings.
Docstring says 'non-empty line' but the line.strip() predicate also leaves whitespace-only lines unindented, so 'non-blank' would be the accurate wording.
Negative spaces silently produces an empty pad and no indentation instead of raising a ValueError like mask_middle does for its analogous parameter.

splitlines() followed by a plain newline join drops any trailing newline and converts every line boundary (CRLF, CR, form feed, U+2028) to LF, so the function mutates the text beyond adding indentation.

O(len(a)*len(b)) time with no input-size guard can stall on large untrusted inputs, and memory could be reduced by using the shorter string for the inner row; complexity is documented, so informational.
